questions.questionText: The ABCDE rule for melanoma detection includes all EXCEPT:
questions.optionB: Benign appearance (B stands for Border irregularity in the ABCDE rule — not 'benign')
questions.explanation: Core concept: ABCDE of melanoma = Asymmetry, Border irregularity, Color variation, Diameter >6mm, Evolution. Why correct: ABCDE criteria for distinguishing melanoma from benign nevi: A = Asymmetry, B = Border irregularity (notched/blurred margins), C = Color variation (multiple shades of brown/black/red/white/blue), D = Diameter >6mm, E = Evolution (changing in size/shape/color). Why wrong: B = Border irregularity, NOT 'benign appearance.' Clinical relevance: Breslow thickness = most important prognostic factor. SLNB for lesions >1mm thick. Clark's levels less used now. Exam trap: E = Evolution is the MOST IMPORTANT single criterion — any changing mole should raise suspicion regardless of size. Memory aid: 'ABCDE = if Any Border Color Diameter Evolves → suspect melanoma.' Reference: IADVL Textbook – Skin Tumors.
